_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-29R Ketamine (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template — MCA Optional.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-29R: Ketamine — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
contraindications: Age restriction for IV/IO: not for patients <6 months
IN route only for patients 6 months to 6 years (no IV/IO route for this age group per protocol)
precautions: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved ketamine for EMS use
Ketamine IV should be diluted to prevent ketamine dissociation
Mix the patient-specific dose into 100 mL NS and administer slow infusion over 5-10 minutes
IM ketamine has a 3-5 minute onset
IN route: administer undiluted using MAD device
notes: Pain management IV/IO: 0.2 mg/kg diluted in 100 mL NS; infuse over 5-10 minutes; maximum 25 mg
Pain management IN: 0.5 mg/kg undiluted via MAD; maximum 50 mg; may repeat once after 10 minutes
Hyperactive delirium syndrome with severe agitation: 4 mg/kg IM; maximum single dose 500 mg
Pediatric pain >6 yr: IV/IO and IN routes per protocol
Pediatric pain 6 months to 6 yr: IN route only (0.5 mg/kg); IV/IO not indicated per protocol for this age group

dosing.dose: 0.2 mg/kg diluted in 100 mL NS; infuse over 5-10 minutes; maximum 25 mg
dosing.notes: Do NOT give as IV bolus; dilute and infuse slowly

dosing.dose: 0.5 mg/kg undiluted via MAD; maximum 50 mg; may repeat once after 10 minutes
dosing.notes: Use MAD; split dose half per nostril
For hyperactive delirium syndrome with severe agitation when patient poses imminent physical threat to self and/or personnel

dosing.dose: 0.2 mg/kg IV/IO (max 25 mg) diluted in 100 mL NS; OR 0.5 mg/kg IN (max 50 mg)

dosing.population: Pediatric 6 months to 6 yr — pain management
dosing.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IN undiluted via MAD; maximum 50 mg
dosing.notes: IN route only for this age group; IV/IO not indicated per Michigan protocol for <6 years pain management

used_in_protocols: Hyperactive Delirium Syndrome with Severe Agitation
